title: Animal tag
abstract: Tag apparatus for attachment to the ear of an animal including a male member with a stem and a penetrating head for penetrating the ear; a portion that abuts against the hack of the ear and cannot be pulled out of the ear, said portion having an end that is not directed towards the head of the animal when the apparatus is fitted to the ear; such that when the apparatus is fitted to the ear, the apparatus has no overhanging material on which the animal can entangle the tag apparatus on a structure.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1 , Tag pp r tus for attae!iment to the ear of an animal ineiud ing : a male member with a stein, and a penetrating head for penetrating the ear; a portion that abuts against the back of the ear and cannot: be pulled out of the ear, said portion having an end that is not directed towards the head of the animal when the apparatus is fitted to the ear; such that "when the apparatus is fitted to the ear, the apparatus has no overhanging material on wh ch the an mal can entangle the tag apparatus on a structure. 2, Tag apparatus according to claim 1 wherein the whole apparatus is made of a hard material having a Shore hardness of 70 to 5D arid more preferabl of 75 to 85D, 3, Tag apparatus according to claim 1 or. claim 2 further including flange means for retaining the tag apparatus flush, on the ear, said flange me ns being positioned against the back of the ear arid behind the stem, 4, Tag apparatus according to any one of the previous claims wherein the portio can move against the back of the ear i two directions of rotation between locations perpendicular to the longitudinal axis of the ear, said portion not having said, end directed towards the head of the animal. 5, Tag apparatus according to claim 1: wherein, said member is deforaiahle such that after penetrating the ear the member or part thereof assumes a shape that retains an identifying means on the ear while not entangling or snagging the tag apparatus on the structure, 6, Tag apparatus according to claim 5 wherein the penetrating head of said member is said portion and said portion extends towards a distal end of the ear and is positioned against the back of the ear, 7. Tag apparatus according to claim 6 wherein the penetrating head is substantially in line with the front and rear edges of the ear and substantially in the middle of the ear. 8. Ta ppar tus according to claim 1 wherein said portion includes a tail portion that is wider at a distal end thereof compared to a roxiraal end of the tail portion adjacen to a stem of said ..member, said proximal end being located nearer to a head of the animal than the distal end. s 9. Tag apparatus according to claim 8 wherein the tail portion tapers outwardly on opposed sides from the proximal end to the distal end. of the tail portion. 10. Tag apparatus according to claim 1 wherein a second portion is provided to receive; said.0 penetrating head and said second portion has means for identifying the animal, 11. Ta apparatus accordin to claim 10 wherein the identifying means ' is a flag or flap on which indicia can be placed to identify the animal said flag or flap generally hanging downwardly torn the inside of the ear, said abutting portion being positionable between 0 5 degrees and 180 degrees with, respect to a longitudinal axis of the ear of the animal with a distal end of the abutting portion not pointing towards the head of the animal. 12. Tag apparatus according to claim 1 1 wherein the portion and the second portion are integrally formed with a line of weakness formed therebetween. 0 13. T g apparatus according to claim 12 wherein, in use, the ear of the animal is placed between the firs and second portions, the first portion is pivoted about the line of weakness and an applicator is used to force the member of the first portio through the ear and be retained in the receiving member of the second portion. 5 14. Tag apparatus according, to claim 1,3 wherein the first portion is separated from the second, portion along the line of weakness prior to in sertion of the penetrating means through t he ear. 0 1 S, Tag apparatus according to any one of claims 8 to 1 wherein the penetrating head has a pair of cutting edges that are used to cut the ear two places to allow for the diameter of the stem and to allow the stem to sit relatively loosely inside the aperture made by the penetrating head in order to insert the tag or a portion of the tag through the ear. 16. A ta for attachment to the ear of an animal including: a m le portion having a penetration member for penetrating the ear of the animal ; a receiving member for receiving and retaining sai penetrating member, such that a portion of the animal ear is lo cated between, the first portion and the second portion; S wherein the male portion has a tail with tapering sides sueh that the tail is wider at a distal end of the tail compared to a proxuiiai end of the tail adjacent the nehating member; siteh that when fitted to the animal ear, the pKMimat end of the tail of the male portion is closer to the head of the animal than the distal end- of the tail of the male portion, so that the animal does, not entangle the tag: on a structure.
